$comment: BD-FIXTURES — GROUND TRUTH for the COLD-START / graceful-degradation paths (release-spec DD-21; the law: 'no seat but a corpus => emit the deterministic analysis + the authoring template for the agent to finish. Never block onboarding.'). Three cases a test asserts. Case A (no corpus, no scraper): the manual authoring path is offered, onboarding proceeds. Case B (corpus present, NO synthesis seat wired): the flow emits the deterministic analysis (expected/analysis.json) AND the authoring template (templates/brand/brand-dna-authoring.md) for the agent to finish — it does NOT fabricate DNA. Case C (corpus + seat wired): full synthesis (expected/brand-dna.expected.md). In every case onboarding is never blocked and nothing auto-publishes. ALL SYNTHETIC. See PROVENANCE.md.

cases.reason: Pure cold start (DD-21). The manual Brand-DNA authoring template is the deliverable; the operator/agent fills it in. Onboarding continues.

Degrade gracefully (DD-21 + RD-2): with a corpus but no host seat, emit the deterministic analysis + archetype catalog + the authoring template for the agent to finish. The engine does NOT call an LLM itself and does NOT fabricate DNA prose.

Full one-command flow: ingest -> deterministic analysis -> archetype catalog -> host-seat DNA synthesis. The two metered actions (scrape, synthesis) each present a pre-run cost estimate + confirm (DD-18) before spending.
